task:
* Don’t rely on `(file, code)` only — ensure **exact string match normalization (strip + whitespace)** is identical to detection path or you risk false orphans.
* In ambiguous branch, **do not append before exit** — ensure no accidental write occurs if exit path changes later.
* Test fixture: **use separate files per case** — avoids hidden coupling with `len(matches) > 1` logic.
* After pruning, assert **no duplicate entries collapse silently** (same file+line+code) — add guard or test.
user_feedback_text:
* Don’t rely on `(file, code)` only — ensure **exact string match normalization (strip + whitespace)** is identical to detection path or you risk false orphans.
* In ambiguous branch, **do not append before exit** — ensure no accidental write occurs if exit path changes later.
* Test fixture: **use separate files per case** — avoids hidden coupling with `len(matches) > 1` logic.
* After pruning, assert **no duplicate entries collapse silently** (same file+line+code) — add guard or test.

task: What do you anticipate the net result of this effort will be on our repo?

The cycle-time tooltip fix itself looks sound, but the commit adds new extension tests without updating the extension collected-test threshold, which
  weakens the CI/preflight guardrail. That regression makes the patch incorrect overall.

  Review comment:

  - [P3] Raise the extension test-count ratchet after adding these Jest cases — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\extension\tests\modules\charts\cycle-
    time.test.ts:236-244
    These four new tests increase the collected count in extension/test-results.xml, but the extension gate still uses --min-collected=2314 in both CI and
    run_pr_preflight.py. That means a future change could accidentally drop up to four extension tests and still pass the “zero tolerance” collection check,
    so this commit weakens the ratchet it is extending.
user_feedback_text:
The cycle-time tooltip fix itself looks sound, but the commit adds new extension tests without updating the extension collected-test threshold, which
  weakens the CI/preflight guardrail. That regression makes the patch incorrect overall.

    These four new tests increase the collected count in extension/test-results.xml, but the extension gate still uses --min-collected=2314 in both CI and
    run_pr_preflight.py. That means a future change could accidentally drop up to four extension tests and still pass the “zero tolerance” collection check,
    so this commit weakens the ratchet it is extending.
